content:
# Revelation Chapter 19 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, pages 291-292 | **Source Image:** REV_019_page_001.jpg, REV_019_page_002.jpg

1. After these [things] I heard as a great voice of a great multitude in the heaven, saying, Hallelujah! The salvation and the glory and the power of the God of us,

2. because true and righteous [are] the judgments of him; because he judged the great harlot, who was corrupting the earth with the fornication of her, and he avenged the blood of the slaves of him from the hand of her.

3. And a second [time] they have said, Hallelujah! And the smoke of her goes up unto the ages of the ages.

4. And the twenty-four elders fell down, and the four living [creatures], and they worshiped the God, the [one] sitting upon the throne, saying, Amen, Hallelujah!

5. And a voice from the throne came out, saying, Praise the God of us, all the slaves of him, and the [ones] fearing him, the small and the great.

6. And I heard as a voice of a great multitude, and as a voice of many waters, and as a voice of strong thunders, saying, Hallelujah! Because [the] Lord the God of us, the Almighty, reigned.

7. Let us rejoice and let us exult, and we will give the glory to him, because the marriage of the Lamb came, and the wife of him prepared herself.

8. And it was given to her that she should clothe herself in fine linen, bright [and] clean; for the fine linen is the righteous [deeds] of the holy [ones].

9. And he says to me, Write: Blessed [are] the [ones] to the supper of the marriage of the Lamb having been called. And he says to me, These the true words of the God are.

10. And I fell before the feet of him to worship him. And he says to me, See [that you do] not! A fellow-slave of you I am and of the brothers of you, the [ones] having the testimony of Yeshua. Worship the God! For the testimony of Yeshua is the spirit of the prophecy.

11. And I saw the heaven having been opened, and behold, a white horse, and the [one] sitting upon it being called Faithful and True, and in righteousness he judges and makes war.

12. And the eyes of him [are] a flame of fire, and upon the head of him many diadems, having a name having been written which no one knows except himself,

13. and having been clothed [in] a garment having been dipped in blood, and the name of him is called The Word of the God.

14. And the armies, the [ones] in the heaven, were following him upon white horses, having been clothed in fine linen, white [and] clean.

15. And from the mouth of him proceeds a sharp sword, that with it he should strike the nations; and he will shepherd them with a rod of iron; and he treads the winepress of the wine of the wrath of the anger of the God, the Almighty.

16. And he has upon the garment and upon the thigh of him a name having been written: King of kings and Lord of lords.

17. And I saw one angel standing in the sun, and he cried out in a great voice, saying to all the birds, the [ones] flying in mid-heaven, Come, gather together unto the great supper of the God,

18. that you may eat flesh of kings, and flesh of commanders, and flesh of strong [ones], and flesh of horses and of the [ones] sitting upon them, and flesh of all, both free and slaves, and small and great.

19. And I saw the beast and the kings of the earth and the armies of them having been gathered together to make the war with the [one] sitting upon the horse and with the army of him.

20. And the beast was seized, and with him the false prophet, the [one] having done the signs before him, by which he deceived the [ones] having received the mark of the beast, and the [ones] worshiping the image of him. Living, the two were cast into the lake of the fire, the [one] burning with sulphur.

21. And the rest were killed with the sword of the [one] sitting upon the horse, the [sword] having come out from the mouth of him, and all the birds were filled from the flesh of them.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **19:1** — Sinaiticus reads "the salvation and the glory and the power" with definite articles before each noun, emphasizing these as specific attributes belonging to God.

2. **19:3** — "a second [time] they have said" — Sinaiticus reads δευτερον ειρηκαν, perfect tense ("they have said"), indicating the ongoing nature of this declaration. Some manuscripts read the aorist (ειπαν).

3. **19:5** — Sinaiticus reads "Praise the God of us" (αινειτε τω θεω ημων) rather than "Praise our God" — the genitive construction is preserved literally.

4. **19:6** — "reigned" (εβασιλευσεν) — aorist indicative, rendered as a simple past. This is the ingressive aorist: "began to reign" or "has taken his reign." EMET preserves the surface tense.

5. **19:8** — Sinaiticus reads "righteous deeds" (δικαιωματα) of the holy ones. Some manuscripts read "saints." The plural "righteous deeds" (not "righteousness") is a distinctive reading.

6. **19:9** — "These the true words of the God are" — the word order follows the Greek emphasis, placing "these" and "true" before the verb for emphasis.

7. **19:12** — "having a name having been written which no one knows except himself" — Sinaiticus preserves this as a name already inscribed yet unknown. The double participial construction is retained.

8. **19:13** — Sinaiticus reads "having been dipped" (βεβαμμενον), perfect passive participle of βαπτω. Some manuscripts and traditions read "sprinkled" (ερραντισμενον). The Sinaiticus reading "dipped" is retained.

9. **19:15** — "the wrath of the anger" (του θυμου της οργης) — Sinaiticus preserves both terms for divine fury. θυμος suggests passionate wrath; οργη suggests settled indignation. Both are retained in their literal force.

10. **19:17** — Sinaiticus reads "one angel" (ενα αγγελον) rather than simply "an angel." The numeral is preserved.

11. **19:20** — "Living, the two were cast" — ζωντες εβληθησαν οι δυο. The participle "living" (ζωντες) is placed first for emphasis: they were cast in while still alive.

— EMET
